name: sim_gerbe_derived_stack_pairwise
claim: Gerbe 2-form curvature (sympy) distinguishes 2-shell vs 3-shell derived-stack configurations; rustworkx DAG nerve structure co-varies non-trivially with shell depth.

pytorch.reason: numeric curvature integration uses numpy; no autograd needed

pyg.reason: DAG structure captured by rustworkx; PyG not needed

z3.reason: admissibility here is a curvature-class comparison, not z3 UNSAT

cvc5.reason: not needed; sympy handles symbolic 2-form comparison

sympy.reason: Constructs the symbolic gerbe 2-form curvature B as a sympy expression and integrates it symbolically over [0,2pi]; the holonomy integral value differs between 2-shell and 3-shell stacks. This symbolic distinction is the load-bearing claim: curvature class inequality proves the configurations are distinguishable, not a numeric approximation.

clifford.reason: Cl(3) bivector e12 used as the natural carrier for gerbe B-field components; per-shell bivector coefficients are summed to get the total curvature in geometric algebra form, cross-validating the sympy symbolic result. Supportive role: confirms numeric bivector sum matches harmonic number pattern.

geomstats.reason: Riemannian metrics not needed; gerbe holonomy is computed algebraically

e3nn.reason: SO(3) equivariance not the focus in this gerbe coupling test

rustworkx.reason: Builds a rustworkx PyDAG representing the derived-stack nerve: each shell adds a node and an edge to the DAG. The DAG node count and longest-path length co-vary with shell depth, and these structural properties are used to distinguish 2-shell from 3-shell configurations independently of the curvature integral. Non-trivial DAG use: dag_longest_path_length is called on a dynamically-constructed graph, not a placeholder.

xgi.reason: hyperedges not needed; DAG captures derived-stack nerve

toponetx.reason: cell complex used in other gerbe sims; DAG is sufficient here

gudhi.reason: persistent homology not required for 2-shell vs 3-shell distinction
